report_name: DentaQuest Suffers Major Breach; ShinyHunters Leaks Data of 2.6 Million

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A prolific, financially motivated extortion group that specializes in large-scale data breaches. Their primary model involves data theft for extortion, followed by the sale or public release of the stolen data if the ransom is not paid. In this incident, they leaked 234 GB of data allegedly stolen from DentaQuest.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for financial extortion and subsequent sale or public release of data.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The initial access vector was not confirmed, but groups like ShinyHunters often exploit vulnerabilities in public-facing web applications or APIs to gain access to backend databases.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exfiltrated a 234 GB database. Hunting hints suggest looking for the execution of database dump commands like 'mysqldump' or 'pg_dump', indicating the collection of data from information repositories.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The group exfiltrated a massive 234 GB archive of data from DentaQuest's network. Monitoring for large, sustained outbound transfers from database servers is recommended for detection.

tools.name: mysqldump
tools.description: The mysqldump utility was mentioned as a likely tool used by the attackers to dump database contents for exfiltration.

tools.name: pg_dump
tools.description: The pg_dump utility was mentioned as a likely tool used by the attackers to dump database contents for exfiltration.

identities.name: DentaQuest
identities.description: A major U.S. dental benefits administrator and a subsidiary of Sun Life. It was the victim of a major data breach where 234 GB of data affecting 2.6 million individuals was stolen and leaked by ShinyHunters.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web forum where the ShinyHunters group published the 234 GB database stolen from DentaQuest.

indicators.name: Database Dump Utility Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Database Dump Utility Execution
id: 7c5b9f3a-1e8d-4f6b-8c4a-9d2e1f7b3a9c
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the execution of common database dump utilities (mysqldump, pg_dump) which can be used by attackers to stage data for exfiltration.'
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-09
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.com/193274/data-breach/dentaquest-breach-shinyhunters-publish-data-impacting-2-6m-people.html
tags:
  - attack.t1213
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mysqldump.exe'
      - '\pg_dump.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of database dump commands like 'mysqldump' or 'pg_dump', which could indicate an attempt to stage data for exfiltration, as suggested in the hunting hints for the DentaQuest breach.
